description: former skyscraper complex in Manhattan, New York (1968-2001)
description_wiki: The original World Trade Center (WTC) was a complex of seven buildings in the Financial District of Lower Manhattan in New York City.

aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.

aliases: Headquarters of the United States Department of Defense
Headquarters of the United States Department of War (2025–present)
Headquarters of the United States Department of Defense (1947–2025)
Headquarters of the United States Department of War (1789–1947)
description: headquarters of the United States Department of Defense; named after the shape of the building, a polygon with five (greek: penta) sides
description_wiki: The Pentagon is the headquarters of the United States Department of Defense, in Arlington County, Virginia, across the Potomac River from Washington, D.C. The building was constructed on an accelerated schedule during World War II. As a symbol of the U.S. military, the phrase The Pentagon is often used as a metonym for the Department of Defense and its leadership.

description: uniformed federal law enforcement agency
description_wiki: The United States Park Police (USPP) is the oldest uniformed federal law enforcement agency in the United States.

description: official residence and office of the President of the United States
description_wiki: The White House is the official residence and workplace of the President of the United States.

description: memorial in Washington, D.C., United States
description_wiki: The Lincoln Memorial is  a U.S. national memorial honoring Abraham Lincoln, the 16th president of the United States, located on the western end of the National Mall of Washington, D.C. The memorial is built in a neoclassical style in the form of a classical temple.

description: memorial in Washington, D.C., United States
description_wiki: The Thomas Jefferson Memorial is a national memorial in Washington, D.C., built in honor of Thomas Jefferson, the principal author of the United States Declaration of Independence and the nation's third president.

description: obelisk on the National Mall in Washington, D.C.
description_wiki: The Washington Monument is a 555-foot (169 m) tall obelisk on the National Mall in Washington, D.C., built to commemorate George Washington, a Founding Father of the United States and the nation's first president.

description: U.S. federal law enforcement agency
description_wiki: The United States Secret Service (USSS or Secret Service) is a federal law enforcement agency under the United States Department of Homeland Security.

entity: Metropolitan Police Department of the District of Columbia
description: law enforcement agency in Washington, D.C., United States
description_wiki: The Metropolitan Police Department of the District of Columbia (MPDC), more commonly known locally as the Metropolitan Police Department (MPD), and, colloquially, DC Police, is the primary law enforcement agency for the District of Columbia, in the United States.

surface_forms: a plane had crashed into the World Trade Center
a plane had struck the Pentagon
that fourth plane crashed in Pennsylvania
the attack on the World Trade Center
description: 2001 attacks in the United States
description_wiki: The September 11 attacks, colloquially known as 9/11, were a coordinated series of suicide attacks perpetrated by the Islamist terrorist organization al-Qaeda against the United States in 2001.

aliases: Rock Creek and Potomac Parkway Historic District
description: parkway in Washington D.C.
principal advisory body to the president of the United States
description_wiki: The Cabinet of the United States is the principal official advisory body to the president of the United States.

entity: United States Secretary of the Interior
description: head of the Department of the Interior in the United States government
description_wiki: The United States secretary of the interior is the head of the United States Department of the Interior.

aliases: National National Parks ServicePark Service, NPS
National United States Parks ServicePark Service of the United States
description: United States federal agency
description_wiki: The National Park Service (NPS) is an agency of the United States federal government, within the United States Department of the Interior.

aliases: the president of the United States
President of the United States of America
description: head of state and head of government of the United States of America
description_wiki: The president of the United States (POTUS) is the head of state and head of government of the United States.

entity: Vice President of the United States
aliases: President of the United States Senate
description: second-highest constitutional office in the United States
description_wiki: The vice president of the United States (VPOTUS, VP, or informally veep) is the second-highest office in the executive branch of the U.S. federal government, after the president of the United States, and ranks first in the presidential line of succession.

aliases: Secretary of State of the United States
description: head of the United States Department of State
description_wiki: The United States secretary of state (SecState) is a member of the executive branch of the federal government of the United States and the head of the U.S. Department of State, equivalent to a minister of foreign affairs.

entity: United States Department of the Interior
surface_forms: Department of Interior law enforcement notification center
description: U.S. federal executive department
description_wiki: The United States Department of the Interior (DOI) is an executive department of the U.S. federal government responsible for the management and conservation of most federal lands and natural resources.

description: 195,000 acres in Virginia (US) maintained by the National Park Service
description_wiki: Shenandoah National Park  (often ) is a national park of the United States that encompasses part of the Blue Ridge Mountains in Virginia.

description: metropolitan area and region of the Philippines

surface_forms: Arlington County and the Pentagon Command Center
description: county in Virginia, United States; part of Washington metropolitan area
description_wiki: Arlington County, or simply Arlington, is a county in the U.S. state of Virginia.

description: U.S. federal law enforcement agency
description_wiki: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) is the domestic intelligence and security service of the United States and its principal federal law enforcement agency.

description: park in Washington, D.C., United States
description_wiki: The Ellipse, sometimes referred to as President's Park South, is a 52-acre (21 ha) park south of the White House fence and north of Constitution Avenue and the National Mall in Washington, D.C., United States.
